appearances: a stout figure with ink-stained fingers and a permanent squint
a tall, angular person whose expensive clothes are slightly too big
a small, quick-eyed individual who never seems to stop moving
someone whose face is arranged into a permanent expression of mild suspicion
mannerisms: constantly straightens objects on any nearby surface
repeats the last word of sentences twice, twice
makes prolonged eye contact as if waiting for you to confess something
laughs too loudly at their own observations
secrets: is fencing goods for a thieves' guild without fully realising it
owes a life debt to someone in this very town
their most valuable item was stolen and replaced with a copy — they haven't noticed yet
is saving to flee the city and start over somewhere no one knows their name

appearances: a broad-shouldered brute whose armour is held together with wire and spite
a lean, scarred fighter who moves with unsettling quiet for their size
someone who looks like they have absorbed several previous adventuring parties
a hulking figure whose eyes suggest they are not entirely present
mannerisms: cracks their knuckles one at a time while speaking
refers to violence as 'sorting things out'
addresses the largest member of the party exclusively
seems almost relieved at the prospect of a fight
secrets: is loyal to their employer out of fear, not conviction
has a family somewhere they send coin to — no one here knows
is looking for an excuse to switch sides
has a genuine phobia that would surprise anyone who met them

appearances: a haggard person with the look of someone who hasn't slept in days
someone whose respectable clothes are rumpled from being slept in
a figure who keeps glancing over their shoulder mid-sentence
someone with the kind of face that's used to smiling, currently failing at it
mannerisms: wrings their hands continuously while speaking
lowers their voice whenever saying anything important
starts sentences three times before finishing one
apologises preemptively for everything they're about to say
secrets: the problem they need solved is partly their own fault
they've already tried hiring someone else — it didn't go well
the reward they're offering is most of what they have left
they know more than they're saying, and know that you know

appearances: a wiry figure with subdermal port scars running up both forearms
someone in a patched envirosuit who clearly lives out of their ship
a person whose left eye is clearly a replacement — a cheaper model than the right
a compact individual with the kind of stillness that comes from spending time in zero-g
mannerisms: runs a thumb over a transaction chip between sentences, a nervous habit
speaks quietly and precisely, as if conserving air
checks the exits before answering any question
refers to everyone as 'friend' in a way that means the opposite
secrets: is moving cargo for someone they've never met in person
their ship's registration doesn't match the hull number
is three payments behind on an augmentation loan they can't default on
knows something about the destination that they're not volunteering

appearances: someone in an unmarked uniform that costs more than your ship
a person whose expression suggests they find you professionally disappointing
a tall, precise individual who writes everything down even when there's nothing to write
someone who looks exactly like what they are, which is somehow the most unsettling thing
mannerisms: records everything without commenting on any of it
asks questions they clearly already know the answers to
has a habit of letting silences run longer than comfortable
uses corporate language so fluently it no longer sounds like words
secrets: their actual orders differ from the ones they've presented
has been building a private file on someone in the crew
disagrees with what they've been sent to do but will do it anyway
is in contact with a party the crew would find surprising

appearances: a small, forgettable person who has turned forgettability into a career
someone nursing a drink they've been making last since noon
a person dressed for a better decade in clothes that haven't caught up
someone whose eyes move more than the rest of them
mannerisms: never faces the door directly but always knows who comes through it
speaks without moving their lips much, an old habit
names a price before answering, even before knowing the question
laughs at nothing, briefly, then stops
secrets: sells to more than one side and keeps them all believing they're the only one
knows something they haven't put a price on yet because they're scared of it
owes someone dangerous a favour they've been putting off
is being watched right now and is hoping you haven't noticed either

appearances: someone whose face you keep failing to fully remember even while looking at it
a person dressed in the fashion of forty years ago, looking entirely comfortable
someone who is standing slightly too still for how long they've been standing
a figure who seems to occupy the room differently than everyone else in it
mannerisms: answers questions with information you didn't ask for and probably needed
refers to recent events in the past tense before they've concluded
knows your name. Does not explain how.
looks at empty spaces in the room as if they aren't empty
secrets: is not entirely present in the conventional sense
has been waiting here for a specific person. May or may not be you.
knows how this ends. Is curious whether knowing would help.
is not what they appear to be, which is itself not what it appears to be
